steps.title: Recognize that both equations describe the same line
steps.content:
Multiply the first equation by 6:

$$6(5x+7y)=6(8)\Rightarrow 30x+42y=48$$

This is exactly the second equation. So the system is really one line: $5x+7y=8$. Any point on that line satisfies both equations.

steps.title: Check each choice against $5x+7y=8$
steps.content: A point $(x,y)$ works for every real $r$ only if substituting it into $5x+7y=8$ always gives $8$. For Choice A — $(r,-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7})$, $x=r$ and $y=-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7}$, so $$5r+7\left(-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7}\right)=5r-5r+8=8.$$ Choice A — $(r,-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7})$ works for all $r$.

steps.title: Eliminate the others
steps.content: Choice B — $(-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7},r)$ gives $\frac{24r+40}{7}$, which is not always $8$. Choices C and D include constants $8$ and $48$, making $5x+7y$ far larger than $8$. Therefore Choice A — $(r,-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{8}{7})$.
